name: Beef and Olive Tagine with Preserved Lemon
description: A slow-cooked Moroccan halal beef tagine with saffron, waxy potatoes, green olives, and preserved lemon. Built on French braising discipline, finished with North African aromatics.
keywords: halal tagine, beef tagine, preserved lemon, halal french cooking, moroccan beef
recipeIngredient: 1 pinch saffron threads, bloomed in 2 tbsp warm water
500 g lean halal beef, diced into 4 cm cubes
700 g waxy potatoes, peeled and cut into wedges
1 large handful green or red olives, rinsed
½ preserved lemon, rind only, quartered
1 handful flat-leaf parsley or coriander, roughly chopped

recipeInstructions.name: Build the base
recipeInstructions.text: In a large heavy casserole over medium heat, warm the olive oil. Add the sliced onion and cook, stirring occasionally, until softened and beginning to colour, about 8 minutes.

recipeInstructions.name: Toast the spices
recipeInstructions.text: Add the grated garlic, ginger, turmeric, black pepper, and salt. Stir continuously for 90 seconds until fragrant.

recipeInstructions.name: Sear the beef
recipeInstructions.text: Add the beef cubes and turn in the spiced onion mixture until all surfaces are coated and starting to colour, about 4 minutes.

recipeInstructions.name: Add liquid and saffron
recipeInstructions.text: Pour in the water and add the bloomed saffron with its soaking liquid. Add the whole chilli if using. Bring to a gentle simmer, cover, and cook for 20 minutes.

recipeInstructions.name: Add the potatoes
recipeInstructions.text: Nestle the potato wedges into the braise. Replace the lid and continue cooking over low heat for 20 to 25 minutes, until potatoes are tender and beef gives easily.

recipeInstructions.name: Finish with olives and preserved lemon
recipeInstructions.text: Add the rinsed olives and preserved lemon rind. Cook uncovered for a final 5 minutes to concentrate the broth.

recipeInstructions.name: Adjust and serve
recipeInstructions.text: Taste for salt, adjusting only after the preserved lemon has cooked in. Scatter fresh parsley or coriander over the top and serve.
